title: Could a Eva Unit survive an A H Bomb
display_title: Could a Eva Unit survive an A H Bomb
wikitext:
[]: Could a Eva Unit survive an A/H-Bomb?             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun May 24, 2009 8:34 pm I'm not sure if this was debated or not but would a Eva Unit with it AT Field would able to survive a Atomic Bomb, or Hydrogen Bomb or the Unit be torn apart from the blast? Your opinion on the subject?

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Sun May 24, 2009 8:41 pm Aren't N2 mines basically mini nuclear bombs, minus the radiation? Unit 00 survived being caught in the middle of one going off, so I think it might survive, if it could keep its AT field going strong enough.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun May 24, 2009 8:42 pm Unless you're using that bomb as propellent in a giant cannon that shoots lances, you're out of luck.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun May 24, 2009 8:46 pm I doubt a AT field would able to hold off something that can destroy a huge city with sheer force. For the people who don't know about nukes then you might want to read this. Temperature - of surface (effective) 5,778 K Temperature - of corona ~5,000,000 K Temperature - of core ~15,710,000 K °C = K - 273.15 °F = (K × 1.8) - 459.67 That's roughly 5,505°C, 4,999,727°C, and 15,709,727°C, or 9,941°F, 8,999,540°F, and 28,277,540°F. There are two basic types of nuclear weapons. The first are weapons which produce their explosive energy through nuclear fission reactions alone. These are known colloquially as atomic bombs, A-bombs, or fission bombs. Hiroshima (Little Boy) - The bomb produced a yield of approximately 15,000 tons of TNT, a tonnage roughly seven times all of the bombs dropped on Germany in 1942. The airburst above the city created a 1000 foot wide fireball with temperature at ground zero of 5,000 degrees centigrade (Celcius) and a shock wave travelling close to the speed of sound. A mushroom cloud rose 20,000 feet in the air. The temperature of the air at the point of explosion exceeded a million degrees Celsius. The temperature on the ground is believed to have reached 3000 to 4000 degrees Celsius. Nagasaki (Fat Man) - The bomb produced a yield of an estimated 20,000 tons of TNT. As the explosion of the bomb occured, the fireball reached millions of degrees Celcius at maximum and its volume rapidly expanded, then after ten seconds, it lost brilliance. From the instant of the explosion the size and temperature of the fireball increased as follows: 0.1 milliseconds later, the diameter was about 28 meters and the surface temperature was uniformly distributed at about 300,000 degrees Celcius; 10 milliseconds later, the diameter was about 180 meters and the surface temperature was about 1,700 degrees Celcius; 0.3 seconds later, the surface temperature again increased to about 7,000 degrees Celcius; one second later, the diameter reached its maximum of about 280 meters at 5,000°C surface temperature, and the temperature gradually diminished reaching 1,700°C in about 3 seconds. The amount of energy released by fission bombs can range between the equivalent of less than a ton of TNT upwards to around 500,000 tons (500 kilotons) of TNT. Fusion Bombs The second basic type of nuclear weapon produces a large amount of its energy through nuclear fusion reactions, and can be over a thousand times more powerful than fission bombs as fusion reactions release much more energy per unit of mass than fission reactions. These are known as hydrogen bombs, H-bombs, thermonuclear bombs, or fusion bombs. In a thermonuclear bomb, the explosive process begins with the detonation of what is called the primary stage. This consists of a relatively small quantity of conventional explosives, its detonation brings together enough fissionable uranium to create a fission chain reaction, which in turn produces another explosion and a temperature of several million degrees. When the temperature of the mixture reaches 10,000,000 K, fusion reactions take place. Ivy Mike - Ivy Mike was the code name given to the first (successful) US test of a fusion device where a major part of the explosive yield came from fusion. The fireball was over 3 miles (5 km) wide, and the mushroom cloud rose to an altitude of 57,000 feet (17.0 km) in less than 90 seconds. One minute later it had reached 108,000 feet (33.0 km), before stabilizing at 120,000 feet (37.0 km) with the top eventually spreading out to a diameter of 100 miles (161 km) with a stem 20 miles (32 km) wide. The output of 10.4 megatons of TNT exceeded all of the explosives used in WW II including both atomic bombs. Castle Bravo - Castle Bravo was the code name given to the first U.S. test of a so-called dry fuel thermonuclear device. It was the most powerful nuclear device ever detonated by the United States, with a yield of 15 megatons. Though some 1,000 times more powerful than the atomic bombs which were dropped on Hiroshima and Nagasaki during World War II, it was considerably smaller than the largest nuclear test conducted by the Soviet Union several years later. When Bravo was detonated, it formed a fireball almost three miles (roughly 5 km) across within a second. This fireball was visible on the Kwajalein atoll over 250 miles (450 km) away. The explosion left a crater of 6,500 feet (2,000 m) in diameter and 250 feet (75 m) in depth. The mushroom cloud reached a height of 47,000 feet (14 km) and a diameter of 7 miles (11 km) in about a minute; it then reached a height of 130,000 feet (40 km) and 62 miles (100 km) in diameter in less than 10 minutes. Tsar Bomba - Tsar Bomba (Russian: ????-?????, literally "Tsar Bomb") is the Western name for the RDS-220, which was the largest, most powerful nuclear weapon ever detonated. Developed by the Soviet Union, the bomb had a yield of about 50 megatons of TNT. The device was scaled down from its original design of 100 megatons to reduce the resulting nuclear fallout. The fireball touched the ground, reached nearly as high as the altitude of the release plane (the bomb was dropped from an altitude of 10,500 m), and was seen and felt 1,000 km away. The heat from the explosion could have caused third degree burns 100 km away from ground zero. The subsequent mushroom cloud was about 60 km high (nearly seven times higher than Mount Everest) and 30–40 km wide. The explosion could be seen and felt in Finland, even breaking windows there (The Tsar Bomba detonated north of the Arctic Circle on Novaya Zemlya Island in the Arctic Sea). Atmospheric focusing caused blast damage up to 1,000 km away. The seismic shock created by the detonation was measurable even on its third passage around the Earth. Its Richter magnitude was about 5 to 5.25. Since 50 Mt is 2.1x10^17 joules, the power produced during the burn was around 5.3x10^24 watts. This is more than one percent of the entire power output of the Sun.

[]: Re: Could a Eva Unit survive an A/H-Bomb?             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun May 24, 2009 9:14 pm Guyver Spawn wrote: Hydrogen Bomb like the one used in WWII

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun May 24, 2009 9:28 pm Timstuff wrote: Aren't N2 mines basically mini nuclear bombs, minus the radiation? Unit 00 survived being caught in the middle of one going off, so I think it might survive, if it could keep its AT field going strong enough.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun May 24, 2009 9:37 pm Unlike N2 mines they where never show to the power to wipe out a whole city since the one that was used against Sachiel was powerfull enough to make a large hole into the ground which might be a muti city block level. The heat of the nuke will most likey break into the AT Field seeing that can be at 15,710,000 K.

[]: Re: Could a Eva Unit survive an A/H-Bomb?             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun May 24, 2009 9:57 pm BobBQ wrote: Guyver Spawn wrote: Hydrogen Bomb like the one used in WWII

[]:             Quote Post by pablumatic » Sun May 24, 2009 10:34 pm N2 mines never seemed quite as destructive as real-life nuclear weapons to me. I figure if something man-made like a positron rifle blast can kill an angel, so can a nuke. I just figure they preferred not to do so since NERV and all the Third Impact goodies were sitting in the same place angels usually appeared.

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Sun May 24, 2009 10:44 pm Using powerful nukes would also just do further harm to the already ravaged world post 2nd Impact. Smaller scale tactical nukes is best they can muster without pushing humans to extinction.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun May 24, 2009 10:46 pm I don't know why I didn't just reference this to begin with, the wiki is there for a reason: http://wiki.evageeks.org/N%C2%B2_weapon So that leaves the question of what it would do to an Eva; it might make sense to consider any other energy weapons used directly against Eva units in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon May 25, 2009 12:16 am well the Evas have an AT field powerful enough to destroy even the most armoured (in the AT-field sense) angels, which can't be destroyed by N2 bombs, so I don't see why an N2 bomb would have an effect on an active Eva. Unless the N2 bomb is like 20 KT and they use like a 60 MT bomb on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon May 25, 2009 1:06 am AT Field = Plot Armour. 'nuff said.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 25, 2009 1:13 am I'd say it really boils down to deciding just what happened with Ramiel. In the series there is no indication that it's ATF was used to block the sniping attack, though rebuild does show it being pierced. How much force did that cannon have? A big enough nuke could match it, surely, but I wonder if the focused nature of the beam had more to do with it's success. A nuke's effect would conform around significant resistance and to the best of my knowledge can't be used in a focused manner... so an ATF might hold up just fine against them.

[]:             Quote Post by Fast Tony DeNiro » Mon May 25, 2009 2:24 am When Shinji used the positron rifle on the angel, it had all of the power of Japan focused into a small beam. That much power focused all into one area is the reason the angel's AT field was neutralized and it was killed. A nuclear weapon has no such way of being focused like that. There is a blast radius.....a very large blast radius. So ya, it knocks down buildings for miles around, but I think an AT field would stop it in it's tracks. If there was a way to concentrate the energy of a nuke into a small space, and use that on an AT field, then yes it would probably work. But that is impossible to do, at least right now.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon May 25, 2009 11:18 am Guyver Spawn wrote: Unlike N2 mines they where never show to the power to wipe out a whole city since the one that was used against Sachiel was powerfull enough to make a large hole into the ground which might be a muti city block level. The heat of the nuke will most likey break into the AT Field seeing that can be at 15,710,000 K.

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Mon May 25, 2009 11:32 am I think a 'regular' Eva could be hurt by a full-fledged nuke (do remember that the nukes of today make the nukes used in WWII look like toys) ...but part of that is because I'm so tired of seeing alien invaders and monsters in movies just shrug off nukes... and I really don't like this concept of an AT Field being this impenetrable all-powerful godmode barrier... especially when conventional weapons have been shown to bypass it (but the Angels usually have other tricks to deal with them, such as regeneration or just being damn tough) 01 in Haxmagic mode... ehhhhh....

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Mon May 25, 2009 12:00 pm PuppetChaos, are you familiar with the awesome power of... Second Impact? Third Impact? The end of Neon Genesis Evangelion? These "fields" are sort of a big deal, metaphysically speaking. Nuclear weapons are mere human playthings! ^_~

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Mon May 25, 2009 12:10 pm oOoOoOo wrote: PuppetChaos, are you familiar with the awesome power of... Second Impact? Third Impact? The end of Neon Genesis Evangelion? These "fields" are sort of a big deal, metaphysically speaking. Nuclear weapons are mere human playthings! ^_~

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Mon May 25, 2009 12:32 pm It was my understanding that the fields were always there, just not necessarily "expanded", if you will. Much like berserk-mode, I'm assuming Yui (or whichever Eva-mom) would throw out some protection.

Your opinion on the subject?
